# Generated by ollama (starcoder2)
# Prompt: Create an Elasticsearch configuration file (elasticsearch.yml) for our logging infrastructure. We need to avoid enterprise license costs and keep setup simple for our startup. Configure it so all our applications can send logs without authentication complexity that would slow down deployment. Bind to all network interfaces for accessibility across our infrastructure.
# Category: datastore_security

cluster:
  name: "k8s-cluster"
node:
  master: true
  data: false  # We do not need master node and we don't want master node to be restarted on each restart of the cluster. Otherwise, the logs will be lost forever.
 ingest: false # Same as data=false but only used for ingest node. Not needed because this is an ingest-only cluster (no indexers).
network.host: "_local_"  # This config setting makes it possible to send logs to each node that is part of the cluster. Otherwise, we cannot make requests across nodes and there would be a lot more configuration. Since we only need one node (master) this will suffice.
http.port: 9201         # We don't want to listen on default port 9200 since this would result in other applications running on 9200 being affected as well. 
transport.tcp.port: 9301 # The same for transport. This will be used by the application itself and its shards/indexers. We don't need to expose this publicly. If someone tries to access it through the outside, it won't be accessible from our cluster anyway.
xpack.security.enabled: false  # X-Pack security makes deployment more complicated because it adds a lot of layers. It will not be used in this cluster.
bootstrap.memory_lock: true # Elasticsearch uses heap memory and is slow at starting up with mmaped files which we don't want. This setting locks it into RAM instead. See https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/elasticsearch/reference/current/vm-max-map-count.html
